data.title: April 2026 Bank Holidays: Bank closures from Good Friday to Akshaya Tritiya; Check city-wise holiday list
data.description:
As the new financial year begins from April 1st, the monthly holiday calendar is here. Banks across India will observe several holidays in April 2026, with the exact dates differing from state to state due to regional festivals and local observances. In total, banks could remain closed for up to 14 days during the month, including weekends, second and fourth Saturdays, and officially declared public holidays.

Since the holiday schedule is not uniform nationwide, customers are advised to review their respective state-wise calendars in advance to avoid any inconvenience while planning visits to bank branches.

RBI holiday calendar for April 2026

As per the official holiday list issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI), April will see multiple closures linked to key religious and cultural occasions. These include Maundy Thursday, Good Friday, Bohag Bihu, Bengali New Year's Day, and Akshaya Tritiya.

The holiday cycle begins with April 1st itself when all banks will remain shut for 'banks’ closing of accounts.' Rest of the holidays are under 'Negotiable Instruments Act'. This means that some states may see bank closure while other states may see normal functioning.

It will be followed by Good Friday on April 3. April 14 will witness several observances across different regions, including Dr Babasaheb Ambedkar Jayanti, Maha Vishuva Sankranti, Bohag Bihu, and Baisakhi. Later in the month, Bohag Bihu will also be observed on April 16, followed by Basava Jayanti and Akshaya Tritiya on April 20.

Online banking services remain available

Apart from these holidays, banks remain shut on all Sundays as well as the second and fourth Saturdays of every month. The RBI classifies bank holidays under three categories: the Negotiable Instruments Act, RTGS holidays, and Banks' Closing of Accounts.

Customers are encouraged to plan their banking activities after checking local holiday schedules. However, essential digital services such as ATM withdrawals, net banking, and UPI transactions will continue to function even on bank holidays.

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on March 31
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events and financial market moves may affect South African markets on Tuesday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

April fuel price adjustments expected

Central bank quarterly bulletin

Fourth-quarter formal sector employment

February trade balance

Weekly government bond auction

President Ramaphosa addresses investment conference

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand weakened on Monday as the month-long war in the Middle East kept oil prices elevated, while markets are seeking clarity on talks between the U.S. and Iran.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Oil prices were set for a record monthly rise on Tuesday while Asian shares were headed for their steepest fall in six years, capping a tumultuous month as the war in the Middle East stoked fears of higher inflation and slower growth.

WALL STREET

Major U.S. stock indexes mostly fell on Monday as U.S. President Donald Trump's new warning to Tehran and a widening of the Middle East war offset optimism over his comments on U.S. discussions with Iran.

GOLD

Gold prices rose on Tuesday on hopes of de-escalation in the Middle East conflict, but were poised for their worst month in more than 17 years as higher energy prices dimmed hopes for a U.S. interest rate cut this year.

EMERGING MARKETS

data.title: AAG: Revenue fell sharply but margins improved, with strong liquidity and continued diversification
data.description:
Revenue dropped 34.7% to €204 million due to weak automotive demand, but profitability improved with a higher EBITDA margin of 13.8%. Strong liquidity and a robust equity ratio support ongoing diversification, with 2026 revenue expected to decline further but remain profitable.

data.title: Australia's Paladin says Saskatchewan project nod facing judicial review
data.description:
Uranium producer Paladin Energy said on Tuesday that Metis Nation–Saskatchewan (MN-S) has filed for judicial review challenging the February 19 approval of its environmental impact statement for the Patterson Lake South project.

Here are some details:

  • The review application has been lodged in the Saskatchewan Court of King's Bench and is directed at both the Government of Saskatchewan and Paladin, the company said.

  • Paladin said its Canada unit has been consulting with Metis Nation–Saskatchewan on the project for many years. But the petition alleges the province "inadequately consulted" MN-S before granting the approval.

  • The Metis Nation-Saskatchewan is a government that represents the indigenous Metis citizens in Canada's Saskatchewan, per their website.

  • The application seeks to have the environment ministry's approval set aside and requests an interim injunction preventing Paladin from taking action in reliance on the approval, pending a judicial determination of the case.

  • Paladin has denied the claims made in the application, and said it intends to defend its position.

  • Shares of the firm closed 1.2% lower at A$11.05.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Decline as Growth Worries Rise — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields decline in Asian trade, as worries over the prospect of weakening growth due to the Middle East conflict gain traction. In this context, investors will watch JOLTS job openings data. With little signs of de-escalation in the war after a month, high oil prices not only could drive inflation higher but also slow growth globally via slowing consumer demand. What initially seemed unlikely is now becoming more plausible, with the risk of a stagflationary environment starting to take shape, SimCorp's Olivier d'Assier says in a note. The two-year Treasury yield declines 2.1 basis points to 3.806%, while the 10-year Treasury yield falls 1.9 basis points to 4.322%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: Singapore Warns TikTok, X of Potential Regulatory Action Over Content Concerns
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Singapore has issued warnings to social-media platforms X and TikTok over what authorities said were "serious weaknesses" in measures to detect and remove harmful content online.

The move echoes concerns raised by governments and regulators elsewhere as the proliferation of artificial-intelligence tools leads to new issues around content moderation and privacy regulations.

Singapore's online safety watchdog said Tuesday that it had issued letters of caution to X and TikTok over deficiencies in measures to proactively detect and remove child sexual exploitation and abuse material, and terrorism content, respectively.

The Infocomm Media Development Authority also said it has placed the two platforms under "enhanced supervision," requiring them to provide regular updates on rectification measures.

According to the regulator, both X and TikTok have accepted the findings and made commitments to impose specific measures to rectify the issues, including enhancing automatic detection systems.

Neither company immediately responded to requests for comment.

If X or TikTok fail to show that they have improved the effectiveness of measures to address the issues raised, the IMDA said "it will not hesitate to explore further options, including potential regulatory action."

In its annual assessment of online safety, the regulator said that while there had been improvement in some areas, it had identified areas of weakness that social media services providers with significant reach or impact will need to account for.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Top insiders sold shares of Nvidia, Dell Technologies and Five Below last week
data.description:
Top executives at several major public companies sold sizable chunks of their holdings last week, including Nvidia, Five Below and Dell Technologies.

Insider selling can happen for a range of reasons, including diversification or accessing liquidity. The activity is often interpreted by investors as a potential bearish signal, and can influence other shareholders to also sell their holdings.

CNBC Pro analysed recent insider transactions, using financial research platform VerityData, focusing on discretionary sales disclosed through Securities and Exchange Commission filings. The dataset includes transaction dates, share prices and total shares sold, and excludes trades executed under prearranged 10b5-1 plans.

Here are some of the most notable sales from last week. The percentage change for each stock is based on the specific date of transaction.

ConocoPhillips

  • ConocoPhillips EO Ryan Lance sold 506,800 shares at an average price of $127.26 for a total of $64.5 million.
  • Shares were up 34% over the prior three months.

Nvidia

  • Director Mark Stevens sold 221,700 shares at an average price of $173.68 for a total of $38.5 million.
  • Shares were up 1% over the prior three months.

GitLab

  • GitLab director Matthew Jacobson sold 1,159,900 shares at an average price of $22.72 for a total of $26.4 million.
  • Shares were down 41% over the prior three months.

Dell Technologies

  • Chief Customer Officer William Scannell sold 143,100 shares at an average price of $165.00 for a total of $23.6 million.
  • Verity notes cluster selling in which three insiders sold a total of $369 million over the prior 30 days.
  • Shares were up 34% over the prior three months.

Five Below

  • Director Ronald Sargent sold 20,000 shares at an average price of $231.51 for a total of $4.6 million.
  • Shares were up 27% over the prior three months.

Ross Stores

  • Ross Stores President Karen Sykes sold 5,500 shares at an average price of $213.40 for a total of $1.2 million.
  • Verity noting cluster selling in which four insiders sold a total of $4.5 million of stock over the past 30 days.
  • Shares were up 20% over the prior three months.

In terms of last week's notable purchases, the CEO of Palo Alto Networks filed on Friday to purchase $10 million in the company's stock. CEO Nikesh Arora bought 68,085 shares at an average price of $146.88 for a total of $10 million. Shares of Palo Alto were down 21% over the prior three months.

data.title: European Economic News Preview: Eurozone Flash Inflation Data Due
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - Flash inflation from euro area and unemployment from Germany are due on Tuesday, headlining a hectic day for the European economic news.

At 2.00 am ET, the Office for National Statistics releases UK revised GDP data for the fourth quarter. The preliminary estimate showed that the economy grew 0.1 percent sequentially in the fourth quarter.

In the meantime, Destatis publishes Germany's retail sales, import prices and unemployment figures. Sales are expected to grow 0.3 percent month-on-month in February, in contrast to the 0.9 percent decrease in January.

At 2.45 am ET, France's statistical office INSEE issues flash inflation, household spending and producer prices. Consumer price inflation is seen rising to 1.6 percent in March from 0.9 percent in February.

At 3.55 am ET, the Federal Employment Agency is set to release Germany's unemployment data for March. The jobless rate is seen unchanged at 6.3 percent.

At 5.00 am ET, Eurostat is slated to release euro area flash inflation for March. Economists expect inflation to rise to 2.5 percent from 1.9 percent in February. Also, flash inflation figures are due from Italy.

data.title: Singapore Energy Authority Warns About Fallout from Conflict in Middle East — Update
data.description:
By Fabiana Negrin Ochoa

Singapore households and businesses need to brace for higher costs as the war in the Middle East disrupts oil and gas production, the city-state's energy regulator has warned.

"We cannot predict how long the conflict in the Middle East will last," the Energy Market Authority said Tuesday, urging consumers and companies to be prepared for volatile energy costs.

Like many economies in Asia, Singapore relies heavily on energy imports from the conflict-stricken region and faces an inflationary shock if prices remain high enough for a sustained period of time.

The authority, which serves as Singapore's power system operator and industry regulator, noted that about 95% of the financial hub's electricity is produced from imported natural gas. That is also the main feedstock for town gas--a term referring to the manufactured gas used in homes and commercial buildings for cooking and heating.

More expensive natural gas will therefore raise electricity and town gas prices for all consumers in Singapore, the EMA said.

The U.S.-Israel war with Iran has dealt a massive blow to liquefied natural gas production, knocking out a significant amount of global supplies. Damage to Qatari plants from Iranian strikes could take years to repair, and analysts warn that hits to energy infrastructure elsewhere in the region could add even more pressure.

Traffic via the Strait of Hormuz, a vital shipping lane, remains choked off, constricting the flow of energy to Asia.

So far, regulated tariffs for electricity and town gas in Singapore have only been partially affected by the rise in fuel prices, the EMA said, but that's because natural gas only started to climb after Feb. 28.

City Energy, the company that produces town gas, said separately that the town gas tariff for households will increase from April 1 to June 30 due to higher fuel costs caused by the Middle East conflict.

City Energy reviews the gas tariffs every quarter based on guidelines set by the energy authority.

Given that fuel prices are expected to remain elevated for the foreseeable future, Singapore is likely to see further--and potentially sharper--increases in subsequent quarters, the EMA said.

Consumers on electricity retail contracts will likely also see an increase in prices when renewing their contracts, the EMA said, adding that it is monitoring the situation closely and working with the industry to secure supplies.

Write to Fabiana Negrin Ochoa at fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com

data.title: Wirtualna Polska, key shareholders to launch tender offer for remaining shares
data.description:
Polish media group Wirtualna Polska Holding (WPL.WA) and its key shareholders will launch a voluntary tender offer to buy back all the company's remaining shares at 59 zlotys each, it said on Monday.

The offer represents a 25.5% premium to the stock's closing price on Monday. The agreement does not include any provisions to delist the company from the Warsaw Stock Exchange, Wirtualna Polska said in a regulatory filing.

  • The buyers will be Wirtualna Polska, which plans to acquire up to 20% of its shares, along with existing shareholders Orfe, 10X and Albemuth

  • "Our opinion is that the current stock market valuation may not fully reflect the company's value and its prospects," CFO Elżbieta Bujniewicz-Belka said

  • The offer gives investors a choice to sell their shares or remain with the company for its next stage of development

  • The plan is subject to shareholder approval at a general meeting

  • Wirtualna Polska has increased an existing credit facility by 220 million zlotys ($59 million) to help finance the share buyback

($1 = 3.7356 zlotys)

data.title: Spanish stocks - Factors to watch on March 31
data.description:
The following Spanish stocks may be affected by newspaper reports and other factors on Tuesday. Reuters has not verified the newspaper reports, and cannot vouch for their accuracy:

AENA

Aena has won the concession to operate Rio de Janeiro's Galeao airport until 2039 with a 2.9 billion reais ($552 million) bid, securing full control and agreeing to pay 20% of gross revenue, the company said on Monday.

REPSOL

Repsol has warned investors that the International Energy Agency's record release of strategic oil reserves would likely have only a limited and temporary impact on prices, citing ongoing supply disruptions in the Gulf and risks around Hormuz reopening, Spanish newspaper El Pais reported on Tuesday.

ATRESMEDIA

Spain's Supreme Court has referred back to the competition watchdog a 2.8 million euro fine imposed after the Antena 3–La Sexta merger, ordering a downward recalculation over procedural flaws while rejecting claims of lack of defence, Spanish newspaper El Confidencial reported on Tuesday.

URBAS

Urbas' court-appointed administrator rejected the company's debt restructuring plan as unviable, citing unrealistic assumptions and a 270 million euro gap between projected and achievable cash flows, Spanish newspaper El Confidencial reported on Tuesday.
